description_long: This object is a large, ground-mounted solar energy installation featuring multiple rectangular solar panels arranged in a staggered, multi-level configuration. The panels are supported by a robust framework, likely made of metal, with several vertical and angled supports visible throughout the structure. The surface of the panels is dark and grid-like, characteristic of photovoltaic cells, while the supporting base appears to be a solid platform with additional structural elements for stability. The installation includes both flat and tilted panels, optimizing sunlight capture from different angles. The overall design suggests it is intended for high-capacity energy generation in an urban or industrial setting, possibly for a solar farm or a large public amenity. No visible cabling or inverter units are apparent from the exterior views.
description: A large, ground-mounted solar energy installation with multiple staggered, rectangular panels on a solid platform and metal supports.
description_view_0: Front view showing the staggered arrangement of solar panels and the solid base platform with visible support structures.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the tilt of several panels and the varying heights of the support framework.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back edges of the panels and the structural supports anchoring them to the base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the angled orientation of the panels and the robust mounting structure.
attribute_panel_orientation: Panels are mostly tilted at approximately 25-35 degrees, with some flat sections; orientation appears fixed and not adjustable.
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with a metal (likely aluminum or steel) frame and angled supports.
attribute_array_configuration: Multiple rows of panels arranged in a staggered, multi-level configuration, approximately 8-10 panels in total.
